laws.title: Public Law 17-132 | Standard Process for International Extraditions Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing extradition statute that operates 'in accordance to 18 U.S. Code §3182' but does not itself amend or replace §3182. No self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Standard Process for International Extraditions Act'.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the 'Standard Process for International Extraditions Act'."

provisions.text_summary: Severability plus anti-supersession clause.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable... This Act shall not be superseded by any newer law..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective on passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall become effective following a majority approval..."

provisions.text_summary: Defines 'extradition'.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new section defining extradition as the removal of a person from one jurisdiction to another for criminal prosecution or punishment.
provisions.evidence: "Extradition shall be defined as the removal of a person..."

provisions.text_summary: Sets extradition requirements, treaty limitations, non-citizen exception, and State/Justice certification.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new section setting the extradition process, including treaty-based limits and DOS/DOJ certification.
provisions.evidence: "In accordance to 18 U.S. Code §3182, the extradition process shall require the following:"
provisions.notes: References §3182 as the governing anchor; no replacement text for §3182 is supplied.

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at Title 50 section 1, but this is an extradition statute tied to Title 18. The source supports a real permanent provision, not a bare note.

recommended_actions: Codify SEC II-III as a new Title 18 section or a statutory note at 18 U.S.C. §3182, not Title 50.
Remove the Trello link from the current note.

laws.title: Public Law 17-133 | Judicial Overhaul Bill (JOB) AMENDED
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Massive Title 28 amendatory act with determinable repeals and rewrites across multiple chapters and sections. No self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as 'Judicial Overhaul Bill' or 'JOB'."

provisions.text_summary: General PL 1-1 applicability instruction.
provisions.evidence: "Pursuant to Public Law 1-1, codes listed in this act shall be applicable section(s) 5,6,7,8,9,10."

provisions.exact_change: Mark each enumerated chapter/section as repealed.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S.Code Chapter 3 shall be repealed, in its entirety."
provisions.notes: Determinable repeal list; duplicate or overlapping targets should be de-duplicated in application.

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites §§133, 134, 135, 136, 137(a), 294, 371, and 413 with replacement text.
provisions.exact_change: Replace each named section with the SEC5 replacement text; resolve §133 against PL 11-72 and §135 against PL 9-52 where referenced.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S.Code § 134 ... shall now read as;"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals §§520, 529, 530A, 530C; Chapters 39 and 40A.
provisions.exact_change: Mark each enumerated section/chapter as repealed.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S.Code §520 shall be repealed."

provisions.exact_change: Mark each enumerated section/chapter as repealed.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S.Code Chapter 41 shall be repealed, in its entirety."
provisions.notes: Chapter 51 is listed twice in the source; apply once.

provisions.exact_change: Mark each enumerated section/chapter as repealed, preserving §1251.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S.Code Chapter 81 shall be repealed, except 28 U.S.Code §1251."
provisions.notes: Chapter 81 / §1251 is duplicated in the source.

provisions.exact_change: Mark each enumerated section as repealed.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S.Code §1656 shall be repealed."
provisions.notes: Overlaps with later repeal activity in other laws.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Chapters 155, 157, 165, and 176.
provisions.exact_change: Mark each enumerated chapter as repealed.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S.Code Chapter 155 shall be repealed, in its entirety."

current_implementation.detail: The current XML has only a boilerplate note at Title 28 section 1. The Act requires many real repeal-markings and textual substitutions; the note cannot substitute for those edits.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to every enumerated Title 28 chapter and section in SEC5-10.
Apply amend-existing-text to §§133, 134, 135, 136, 137(a), 294, 371, and 413 using the supplied replacement language.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-134 | Amended The Uniformed Capitol Police Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Operative amendment to PL 16-119, Section III(B), specifying Capitol Police Board membership. It is not a numbered U.S. Code amendment and it is not self-repealing.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the 'Amended The Uniformed Capitol Police Act of 2023'."

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 16-119 Section III(B) to set the Capitol Police Board membership list.
provisions.exact_change: Set PL 16-119 §III(B) to the quoted membership list in the act.
provisions.evidence: "Section III, Subsection B shall now read the following:"
provisions.notes: Apply the amendment wherever PL 16-119 is carried in the code or statutory history.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note sits at Title 18 section 1 and labels the law 'Repealed', but the source is an operative amendment to PL 16-119. The amendment must be carried into PL 16-119's text.

recommended_actions: Apply the SEC II amendment to PL 16-119 Section III(B) wherever it is carried.
Remove the unsupported 'repealed' classification.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-135 | Judicial Bias Protection Act (JBPA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Section 5 determinably rewrites 28 U.S. Code §144, and Sections 6-7 create new permanent judicial-procedure rules. No self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as 'Judicial Bias Protection Act' or 'JBPA'."

provisions.text_summary: Defines 'dereliction of duties'.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new definition section for 'dereliction of duties'.
provisions.evidence: "'dereliction of duties' means a judge's failure to perform their obligations..."

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites 28 U.S. Code §144 (bias/prejudice recusal, affidavit review, reassignment, and 15-day bar).
provisions.exact_change: Replace §144 with the SEC5 text.
provisions.evidence: "28 U.S.Code §144 shall now read as-"
provisions.notes: The heading is OCR-noisy, but the section target is clear.

provisions.text_summary: Hearing-notice and scheduling rules for federal judges.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new procedural section governing notice, pre-trial timing, waiver, and remedies.
provisions.evidence: "Any federal judge presiding over a case must allow each party up to at least twenty-four (24) hours..."

provisions.text_summary: Bench-inactivity protection; impeachment liability for judges who take no cases within 20 days without notice.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new section imposing the bench-inactivity rule and impeachment consequence.
provisions.evidence: "shall be liable for impeachment for dereliction of duty if they shall not have taken any cases within the last twenty (20) days..."

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is only a boilerplate note at Title 28 section 1. The required §144 textual amendment and the new procedural sections were not actually enacted in the XML.

recommended_actions: Amend 28 U.S.C. §144 with the SEC5 replacement text.
Codify SEC6-7 as new Title 28 procedural sections.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-136 | The Parks Act (TPA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Grants Secret Service primary authority over the President's Park and sets closure/enforcement rules. No self-repeal or sunset; the source supports operative law, not a repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as 'The Parks Act' or 'TPA'."

provisions.text_summary: Secret Service primary authority over President's Park and White House grounds list.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new protective-authority section listing the White House grounds and the Secret Service's primary authority.
provisions.evidence: "The Secret Service shall have primary authority over the entirety of the President's Park..."

provisions.text_summary: Permanent closure rules, emergency closures, public notice, and 48-hour limit.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new section governing closures, notices, and duration limits for the listed areas.
provisions.evidence: "E-Street Closed, State Place, Hamilton Place, EisenHower Executive Office Building, Treasury Department, The White House shall be permanently closed..."

provisions.text_summary: Other law-enforcement secondary authority and entry/closure enforcement rules.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new section describing secondary law-enforcement authority and Secret Service entry permission rules.
provisions.evidence: "Law enforcement agencies, except the Secret Service, shall have secondary authority over the President's Park."

current_implementation.detail: The draft note is anchored in Title 54 section 100101(b)(2) and falsely labels the law repealed. The source supports an operative protective-authority law, so the note is misanchored and misclassified.

recommended_actions: Remove the unsupported repeal claim.
Codify the Secret Service authority and closure rules in a more apt protective-authority location rather than a bare Title 54 note.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-137 | Federal Background Check Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing federal-employment background-check regime with no numbered-Code amendment and no self-repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the 'Federal Background Check Act'."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions of background check and federal agencies.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new definitions section for background check and federal agencies.
provisions.evidence: "Background Check shall be defined as the collection and verification of an individual's information..."

provisions.text_summary: All federal agencies, including the military, must use FBI-approved background checks; FBI conducts and maintains them.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new section imposing the agency-wide FBI background-check requirement.
provisions.evidence: "All Federal Agencies are to comply with each employee passing a background check approved and signed by the FBI."

provisions.text_summary: Future federal employment requires a background check; leadership is not exempt; AG waiver and timing rules apply.
provisions.exact_change: Create a new section making background checks mandatory for future federal employment.
provisions.evidence: "the Federal Background Check shall be a requirement in order to acquire employment inside any federal agency..."

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date.
provisions.evidence: "This bill will take effect immediately after passage..."

current_implementation.detail: The XML note is at Title 50 section 1 and declares the law repealed. The source shows no repeal; the background-check mandate belongs in Title 5, not Title 50.

recommended_actions: Codify SEC III-IV as a new Title 5 employment-requirement section.
Remove the unsupported repealed status.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-138 | Hatch Act Amendment Act (HA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Section 5 repeals 5 U.S. Code §7323 and immediately re-enacts it with determinable replacement text. No self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as the 'Hatch Act Amendment Act' or 'HA'."

provisions.text_summary: Defines 'employee' for the §7323 rewrite.
provisions.evidence: "the term 'employee' means any individual who is currently employed by the federal government..."

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 5 U.S. Code §7323 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal §7323, then apply the SEC5(2) replacement text.
provisions.evidence: "5 U.S.Code §7323 shall be repealed in its entirety."

provisions.text_summary: Re-enacts 5 U.S. Code §7323 with political-activity restrictions and exceptions.
provisions.exact_change: Replace §7323 with the SEC5(2) text.
provisions.evidence: "5 U.S.Code §7323 shall now read as-"

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note sits at Title 5 section 101 and does not perform the required repeal-and-replace of §7323.

recommended_actions: Amend 5 U.S.C. §7323 with the SEC5(2) replacement text after repeal-marking.
Move the note anchor to §7323 and remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 18-139 | The Bye Bye Background Checks Act
laws.status_basis: Source unrecoverable. law.txt is a Google Drive access-denied page, so no operative repeal text can be authenticated. The title suggests a repeal of the background-check regime, but that cannot be confirmed from the supplied source.

provisions.text_summary: No statutory text recoverable; only a Drive access-denied page was captured.
provisions.evidence: "Sorry, the owner hasn't given you permission to download this file."
provisions.notes: Source-limited; the manifest's dump reference is to unrecoverable content.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note includes an improper quoted-content dump and a Trello link while declaring the law repealed. The true issue is source unavailability, not a confirmed repeal.

recommended_actions: Retain only a source-limited historical note pending authentic source recovery.
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
If authentic text is recovered and it repeals PL 17-137, apply repeal-marking to that regime.

provisions.text_summary: Foreign Service Act PDF whose body text is not present in the extraction; the viewer blocked extraction.
provisions.evidence: "Printing is disabled for this file"
provisions.notes: Viewer debris only; likely Title 22 material if recovered.
